title: A New Hope: How CAR T-Cell Therapy Is Changing Cancer Treatment
subtitle: Scientists are using advanced technology to help the body's immune system fight cancer more effectively.

summary.en: A new medical treatment called CAR T-cell therapy is showing great promise in fighting cancer. The process involves changing a patient's own immune cells to better find and destroy cancer cells. While it is currently expensive and mostly used for blood cancers, researchers are working to improve it. Famous actor Sam Neill recently shared his success with this treatment. Experts believe we are only at the beginning of what this technology can achieve.
summary.zh: 一種稱為「嵌合抗原受體T細胞療法」的新型醫療方式，在對抗癌症方面展現了巨大潛力。此過程涉及改造患者自身的免疫細胞，使其能更有效地發現並摧毀癌細胞。雖然目前該療法費用昂貴且主要用於血液癌症，但研究人員正致力於改善技術。知名演員山姆·尼爾近期分享了他透過此療法康復的經歷。專家認為，我們才剛開始發掘這項技術的成就潛力。

paragraphs.en: In the world of medicine, scientists are celebrating a major step forward in the fight against cancer. A treatment known as CAR T-cell therapy is gaining attention for its ability to help the body’s own immune system attack disease. According to The Guardian Health, experts are calling this a "game-changer" for patients who have not found success with other treatments.
paragraphs.zh: 在醫學領域，科學家們正慶祝對抗癌症的一大進展。一種稱為「嵌合抗原受體T細胞療法」（CAR T-cell therapy）的治療方式，因其能協助人體自身的免疫系統攻擊疾病而備受矚目。根據《衛報健康》（The Guardian Health）報導，專家們稱這對於其他治療無效的患者而言，是一項「改變遊戲規則」的突破。

paragraphs.en: To understand how this works, we must first look at the immune system. Our bodies have a natural defense system that fights off bacteria and viruses. A key part of this system is the T-cell, a type of white blood cell that acts like a soldier. These cells look for threats and destroy them. However, cancer cells are very clever. They often hide from the immune system, making it hard for the body to fight the disease on its own.
paragraphs.zh: 要了解其運作原理，必須先從免疫系統談起。人體擁有天然的防禦系統來對抗細菌與病毒。此系統的核心部分是T細胞，這是一種如同士兵般的白血球。這些細胞會搜尋威脅並將其消滅。然而，癌細胞非常狡猾，它們經常躲避免疫系統，導致身體難以獨自對抗疾病。

paragraphs.en: CAR T-cell therapy is designed to solve this problem. The process begins by taking a patient's T-cells from their blood. Scientists then change these cells in a laboratory. They add a special "GPS" to the cells, which helps them find the exact location of the cancer. Once these modified cells are grown in large numbers, they are put back into the patient’s body. Once inside, these "supercharged" cells hunt and destroy the cancer. Prof Misty Jenkins, an immunologist at the Walter and Eliza Hall Institute of Medical Research, explains that this is like giving the immune system a better map to find its target.
paragraphs.zh: 嵌合抗原受體T細胞療法正是為了克服此問題而設計。治療過程始於從患者血液中提取T細胞，科學家隨後在實驗室中對這些細胞進行改造，為其添加特殊的「全球定位系統」，協助它們精準定位癌細胞的位置。一旦這些改造後的細胞大量增殖，便會被輸回患者體內。進入體內後，這些「升級版」細胞便會追蹤並摧毀癌症。沃爾特與伊麗莎霍爾醫學研究所（Walter and Eliza Hall Institute of Medical Research）的免疫學家米斯蒂·詹金斯（Misty Jenkins）教授解釋，這就像是給免疫系統一張更精確的地圖，以利找到目標。

paragraphs.en: This treatment recently gained public attention when actor Sam Neill shared his personal story. After being diagnosed with stage three cancer, he took part in a clinical trial in Sydney. He later announced that his cancer was in remission, meaning the signs of the disease had disappeared. He described the success as "science at its best," noting that it was not a miracle, but the result of hard work and research.
paragraphs.zh: 此療法近期因演員山姆·尼爾（Sam Neill）分享其個人經歷而受到大眾關注。他在確診第三期癌症後，參加了雪梨的一項臨床試驗。隨後他宣布癌症已進入緩解期，意即疾病跡象已消失。他將此成功描述為「科學的極致展現」，並指出這並非奇蹟，而是辛勤研究的成果。

paragraphs.en: While this technology is exciting, it is important to understand its current limits. The Guardian Health reports that CAR T-cell therapy is still very expensive. Currently, it is mostly used for blood cancers, such as leukemia or lymphoma. Researchers are now working hard to see if this method can be used to treat solid tumors, which are harder to reach. While early tests show promise, scientists are still learning how to make the T-cells more effective at finding these different types of cancer.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管這項技術令人振奮，但了解其目前的侷限性相當重要。《衛報健康》報導指出，嵌合抗原受體T細胞療法目前仍非常昂貴。現階段，它主要用於治療白血病或淋巴瘤等血液癌症。研究人員正致力於探討此方法是否能用於治療較難觸及的實體腫瘤。雖然早期測試顯示出潛力，但科學家仍在研究如何讓T細胞在尋找這些不同類型的癌症時更具效率。

paragraphs.en: Compared to traditional treatments like chemotherapy—a common medical treatment that uses strong drugs to kill cancer cells—CAR T-cell therapy has some clear differences. Chemotherapy is often cheaper and can treat a wider range of cancers. Dr. Criselle D’Souza, an immunologist at the Peter MacCallum Cancer Centre, noted that chemotherapy remains a main pillar of cancer care because doctors know its side effects well and can manage them effectively. However, CAR T-cell therapy has one major advantage: it can provide long-term protection. In the best cases, the modified cells stay in the body for a long time, which helps prevent the cancer from coming back.
paragraphs.zh: 與傳統治療（如化學治療，即使用強效藥物殺死癌細胞的常見醫療手段）相比，嵌合抗原受體T細胞療法有明顯差異。化學治療通常較便宜，且能治療更廣泛的癌症。彼得·麥卡勒姆癌症中心（Peter MacCallum Cancer Centre）的免疫學家克莉絲兒·德索薩（Criselle D’Souza）博士指出，化學治療仍是癌症照護的主要支柱，因為醫師對其副作用相當了解，且能有效管理。然而，嵌合抗原受體T細胞療法有一項重大優勢：它能提供長期保護。在最佳情況下，改造後的細胞會長期留在體內，有助於防止癌症復發。

paragraphs.en: Looking to the future, scientists are optimistic. The history of this therapy shows how small discoveries over many years can lead to big changes in patient care. Since 2018, several types of CAR T-cell therapies have been approved for use in Australia. Researchers are now looking for ways to make the process faster and more accessible. There is even talk that one day, the treatment could be given as a simple injection rather than a complex hospital procedure.
paragraphs.zh: 展望未來，科學家們持樂觀態度。此療法的歷史顯示，多年來的小發現如何能為患者照護帶來重大改變。自2018年起，已有數種嵌合抗原受體T細胞療法在澳洲獲准使用。研究人員目前正尋求讓治療過程更快速且更普及的方法。甚至有討論指出，未來該療法或許能像簡單的注射一樣，而非複雜的醫院療程。

paragraphs.en: Despite the challenges, the mood among medical professionals is one of excitement. Prof Misty Jenkins stated that we have only just "scratched the surface" of what is possible with this technology. As scientists continue to refine the process, they hope to turn this advanced therapy into a standard weapon against many different forms of cancer. For now, the success stories of patients like Sam Neill offer real hope to those waiting for new ways to fight this difficult disease.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管面臨挑戰，醫療專業人員之間仍充滿興奮之情。米斯蒂·詹金斯教授表示，我們對這項技術的潛力才剛「觸及皮毛」。隨著科學家持續改良流程，他們希望將這種先進療法轉變為對抗多種癌症的標準武器。目前，像山姆·尼爾這樣的患者成功案例，為那些正等待新方法對抗此頑疾的人們提供了真正的希望。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To defend oneself against or successfully resist a disease or attacker.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見於打鬥情境，這裡指身體對抗細菌。文中：Our bodies have a natural defense system that fights off bacteria and viruses.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A fundamental or essential part of a system or organization.
keyTerms.noteZh: 原意為建築物的柱子，這裡比喻醫療體系中不可或缺的治療方式。文中：Dr. Criselle D’Souza, an immunologist at the Peter MacCallum Cancer Centre, noted that chemotherapy remains a main pillar of cancer care because doctors know its side effects well and can manage them effectively.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To deal with only a very small part of a subject or problem.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 僅觸及皮毛、剛開始接觸（某事物的表面）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 字面意思是抓表面，實際指對某領域的潛力才剛開始探索。文中：Prof Misty Jenkins stated that we have only just "scratched the surface" of what is possible with this technology.

sources.name: The Guardian Health
sources.title: The emerging cancer treatment that’s exciting scientists: ‘We’ve just scratched the surface on what’s possible’

quiz.question: What is the primary function of the laboratory modification performed on T-cells during CAR T-cell therapy?

options.A: To increase the total number of white blood cells in the patient's body.
options.B: To equip the cells with a mechanism that allows them to locate cancer cells more accurately.
options.C: To replace the patient's immune system with a stronger, artificial version.
options.D: To change the physical shape of the cells so they can survive chemotherapy drugs.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，科學家在實驗室中對 T 細胞進行改造，添加了一個特殊的「GPS」，其目的是幫助細胞精確定位癌症的位置。選項 A 錯誤，雖然細胞會被大量培養，但改造的目的並非單純增加數量；選項 C 錯誤，療法是增強既有免疫系統而非替換；選項 D 錯誤，文中並未提及改變細胞形狀以抵抗化療藥物。

quiz.question: Based on the information provided, why might chemotherapy remain a more common treatment option than CAR T-cell therapy for many patients?

options.A: Chemotherapy is more effective at curing solid tumors than CAR T-cell therapy.
options.B: CAR T-cell therapy is only available to famous individuals like Sam Neill.
options.C: Chemotherapy is more affordable and its side effects are better understood by medical professionals.
options.D: CAR T-cell therapy is considered a 'miracle' cure that scientists do not fully understand yet.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第六段，雖然 CAR T-cell 療法有其優勢，但文中明確指出化療目前仍是癌症治療的主要支柱，原因在於其價格相對便宜，且醫生對其副作用有充分的了解與管理經驗。選項 A 錯誤，文中提到化療治療範圍廣，但未說它對固態腫瘤更有效；選項 B 錯誤，文中僅以 Sam Neill 為例，未限制使用對象；選項 D 錯誤，文中提到這是科學研究的成果，而非無法解釋的奇蹟。

quiz.question: In the final paragraph, what does the phrase 'scratched the surface' mean in the context of Prof Misty Jenkins' statement?

options.A: Scientists have only begun to discover the full potential of this technology.
options.B: The therapy is only effective on the outer layer of the skin.
options.C: Researchers are currently struggling to find any positive results.
options.D: The technology has reached its maximum capability and cannot be improved further.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。片語「scratched the surface」意指「僅觸及皮毛」或「剛開始接觸」，在文末語境中，意指這項技術的潛力巨大，目前取得的成就僅是冰山一角，未來還有更多發展空間。選項 B 為字面誤解；選項 C 與文中對科學家樂觀態度的描述矛盾；選項 D 的意思則與片語含義完全相反。

quiz.question: Which statement best summarizes the central message of the article?

options.A: CAR T-cell therapy is a dangerous procedure that should be avoided in favor of traditional chemotherapy.
options.B: The success of CAR T-cell therapy depends entirely on the financial status of the patient.
options.C: While CAR T-cell therapy faces challenges, it represents a promising and evolving advancement in cancer treatment.
options.D: Cancer research has reached its final goal, and no further improvements are needed in medical science.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。本文全面介紹了 CAR T-cell 療法的運作原理、優勢（如長期保護）、現有的限制（高昂成本、僅限血癌）以及對未來的樂觀展望。選項 A 與文意不符，文章持正面態度；選項 B 過度簡化，文中雖提成本但非唯一重點；選項 D 與文末提到的「持續改進」及「還有很多可能」相違背。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
